{"query":"Notre-Dame Paris Claire Tabouret stained glass windows installation status 2026 opposition","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1350,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/06/notre-dame-cathedral-claire-tabouret-art-windows","title":"‘It’s not very French to change stuff’: how Claire Tabouret’s stained-glass windows cast Notre Dame in new light | Notre Dame | The Guardian","content":"Before she was chosen from more than 100 artists to design six new stained-glass windows for the cathedral – reopened in 2024, five years after it almost burned to the ground – Tabouret had a select group of admirers (one of them the French tycoon and art collector François Pinault), but she was hardly a household name.
In Paris, Tabouret’s window designs are on display at the Grand Palais, before being installed at Notre Dame later this year at an estimated cost of €4m (£3.3m).
When it was announced, the notion of creating something new, which meant replacing the undamaged windows in six chapels on the medieval cathedral’s southern aisle, caused a furore.
Her windows are being made at the Atelier Simon-Marq, a nearly 400-year-old glass workshop founded in Reims that has previously worked with artists such as Marc Chagall and Joan Miró."},{"url":"https://nwcatholic.org/news/caroline-de-sury-osv-news/new-stained-glass-designs-for-notre-dame-now-on-display-amid-ongoing-debate","title":"New stained-glass designs for Notre Dame now on display amid ongoing debate - Northwest Catholic: Read Catholic News & Stories","content":"The works, designed by French artist Claire Tabouret, are featured in the exhibition “In One Breath” at the Grand Palais through March 15.
The stained-glass windows are currently being made in Reims, in France’s Champagne region.
They are to be installed by the end of the year in the iconic cathedral, which reopened in December 2024 after the fire in April 2019.
The stained-glass project has sparked much controversy since its announcement in 2023 by President Emmanuel Macron.
It was a statement from the Presidency of the Republic that announced the choice of Tabouret on Dec.
Once installed at Notre Dame, the new stained-glass windows will cover an area of 1,300 square feet, or just under 5% of the cathedral’s 27,000 square feet of stained-glass windows from all periods."},{"url":"https://www.judsonstudios.com/blog/blog-3/notre-dame-contemporary-stained-glass-claire-tabouret-39","title":"Notre Dame Stained Glass Controversy: Claire Tabouret & Judson Studios","content":"These new designs have sparked significant controversy in both artistic and preservationist circles.
Not all contemporary glass installations in historic spaces succeed, but I believe this project will.
Notre Dame’s new windows evoke earlier debates at the Cathédrale de Tours and Westminster Abbey."},{"url":"https://www.alminerech.com/news/11941-the-grand-palais-in-paris-announced-a-major-solo-exhibition-by-claire-tabouret-showcasing-her-preparatory-works-for-the-six-stained-glass-windows-of-notre-dame-cathedral","title":"The Grand Palais in Paris announced a major solo exhibition by Claire Tabouret, showcasing her preparatory works for the six stained-glass windows of Notre-Dame Cathedral - Almine Rech","content":"The artist, in association with Atelier Simon-Marq, is the winner of the competition organized by the French Ministry of Culture to design the windows for Notre-Dame Cathedral in Paris.
Claire Tabouret's proposal was selected at the end of the second phase of the competition launched by the Ministry of Culture in March 2024."},{"url":"https://www.artprice.com/artprice-news/12512/controversy-over-claire-tabouret-s-stained-glass-windows-at-notre-dame-in-paris-heritage-artistic-and-political-issues","title":"Controversy over Claire Tabouret’s stained‑glass windows at Notre‑Dame in Paris: heritage, artistic and political issues","content":"The controversy surrounding the future stained-glass windows by Claire TABOURET (1981) for Notre-Dame de Paris crystallizes artistic, heritage-related and political issues, reignited by the presentation to the public of six monumental full-scale cartoons at the Grand Palais in early December 2025.
In public statements, the commission has been presented by the authorities as a gesture of unity, both spiritual and social, intended to root Notre-Dame in the present without breaking with its liturgical dimension.
Discover the exhibition Eva Jospin, Grottesco · Claire Tabouret, D’un seul souffle, at the Grand Palais, Paris, until 15 March 2026."}]}

She designed six contemporary stained-glass windows for Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris.
